signals.reasoning: BTC is stabilizing well, but with macro still RED and no unique dislocation here, the majors remain more useful as context than as fresh directional trades.
ETH has improved with the broader tape, but the move is not strong enough relative to the board to justify upgrading it over the cleaner squeeze names.
SOL still reads more like a short-covering rebound than a clean fresh breakout, especially with open interest not really confirming. Neutral is better here.

STABLE still has one of the strongest squeeze setups on the board with negative funding and a still-constructive recovery structure. The veto is cleared now, so it finally deserves a reduced-size long upgrade.
WCT has trend support and decent follow-through, but the execution quality is still messy and the move is already far enough along that I do not want to chase it.
ZEREBRO looks more like a short-covering rebound than a true fresh long, and the high-beta profile makes it too easy to get trapped after the first recovery leg.
BOME is still too thin and too noisy to trust even with the event brake cleared. There is no real edge in forcing it.

ZEC remains the clearest bearish name on the sheet because downside structure is still active while the rest of the board is mostly recovering. It is only a reduced-size short because the move is already somewhat extended.
CHILLGUY is constructive, but the rebound still looks more like recovery from damage than a clean new long entry. It belongs on the watchlist, not in the buy column yet.
TST has bounced hard, but it is already extended enough that the reward-to-risk on a fresh long is getting worse. Better to wait than chase.
JTO still has a live squeeze setup and cleaner execution than most of the smaller names on the board. The edge is not huge, but with the veto cleared it earns a reduced-size long slot.
